title: Response Bytes, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity · Program Brief: Compressed Byte Budget
summary: The parser receives truncated compressed bytes, guesses the platform encoding, ignores a conflicting declaration, and hashes normalized text as if it were the source. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the compressed byte budget increment in byte-to-Unicode admission ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.
Enforce compressed and expanded byte limits during streaming, close partial bodies, and retain a visible rejection reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-compressed-byte-budget — Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-bytes-encoding-admission-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-bytes-encoding-admission-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_bytes_encoding_admission_4(wire_bytes: int = 48000, expanded_bytes: int = 120000, byte_budget: int = 250000, decoded_pages: int = 6, visible_decode_failures: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if wire_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < wire_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "byte-accounting"
    if expanded_bytes > byte_budget:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "expansion-budget"
    if decoded_pages < 1:
        return False, decoded_pages, "decode-coverage"
    if visible_decode_failures < 1:
        return False, visible_decode_failures, "decode-evidence"
    assert expanded_bytes <= byte_budget
    return True, expanded_bytes, "bounded-decoding-ledger"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-compressed-byte-budget-1fq62r
# Competency: scraper-compressed-byte-budget.
# Case 4745: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_compressed_byte_budget_1fq62r(wire_bytes: int = 48000, expanded_bytes: int = 120000, byte_budget: int = 250000, decoded_pages: int = 6, visible_decode_failures: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1fq62r = "1fq62r-r26qf1-4745"
    assert evidence_variant_1fq62r
    evidence_axes_1fq62r = (
        "authorization1fq62r",
        "url4745",
        "responser26qf1",
        "tree1fq62r4745",
        "frontier47451fq62r",
        "reportr26qf14745",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1fq62r)) == 6
    if wire_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < wire_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "byte-accounting"
    if expanded_bytes > byte_budget:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "expansion-budget"
    if decoded_pages < 1:
        return False, decoded_pages, "decode-coverage"
    if visible_decode_failures < 1:
        return False, visible_decode_failures, "decode-evidence"
    assert expanded_bytes <= byte_budget
    return True, expanded_bytes, "bounded-decoding-ledger"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Compressed Byte Budget structural check is only one gate. byte-to-Unicode admission ledger must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Build Compressed Byte Budget evidence · Theory Compressed Byte Budget, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Compressed Byte Budget precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Build Compressed Byte Budget evidence · Theory Compressed Byte Budget, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 5395 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.

For Authorized crawling and Python Build Compressed Byte Budget evidence · Theory Compressed Byte Budget, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: A small compressed response is necessarily cheap to decode.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Compressed Byte Budget model · Theory Compressed Byte Bud…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Compressed Byte Budget: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 4160 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Compressed Byte Budget model · Theory Compressed Byte Bud….
steps.why: Retrieving Compressed Byte Budget through the concrete byte-to-Unicode admission ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Compressed Byte Budget model · Theory Compressed Byte Bud…, keep Compressed Byte Budget connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.
